The '''''Chu'unthor''''' (the [[Cerean (language)|Cerean]] word for "academy") was a [[Praxeum ship/Legends|praxeum ship]] designed by Cerean [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Fin-So-Rowan/Legends|Fin-So-Rowan]] and [[Human/Legends|Human]] Jedi [[Ivixa Delbaeth]]. The ship was built in orbit around the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Abhean/Legends|Abhean]] by [[Republic Fleet Systems]] in [[400 BBY/Legends|400 BBY]].

==History==
[[File:Chuunthor-JATM.jpg|left|thumb|''Chu'unthor'' in planetary orbit]]

Its primary function was as a spacefaring academy for groups of [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] apprentices, and it operated under the command of a number of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] before crashing on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] in [[230 BBY/Legends|230 BBY]]. The Academy aboard the ''Chu'unthor'' produced several powerful Jedi, including [[Easal Mar]] and [[Ainvar ul-Esca]]—the latter later pursued a mission of diplomacy and exploration, negotiating a truce on [[Qualtrough]], and making first contact with the [[Elathan]].

The ship was quite large: it measured two kilometers long, one kilometer wide, and at least forty meters tall. A command pinnacle in the center of the ship housed the majority of the non-Jedi crew, with the [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] and [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] engine array mounted directly aft. The ''Chu'unthor'' could accommodate 10,000 Jedi students, and contained hundreds of exercise and lightsaber-sparring rooms to hone each student's concentration, form, and body. Workshops were provided for the construction and maintenance of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], while meditation chambers gave students and masters alike a place to contemplate the nature of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and further their mental discipline. A medical wing contained hundreds of rooms used to teach Jedi healing arts to young students, both for use in the aftermath of battle or accident and to aid the physically or mentally impaired. The ship contained its own extensive library with texts covering the myriad of Jedi teachings, advanced and theoretical science tomes, historical annals, literature from across the galaxy, and encyclopedias covering the galaxy's languages, art, and culture.

The ship had a relatively small crew, due to much of the ship being automated and run by droids. Most of the crew were [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] veterans. A number of non-Jedi research organizations, including the [[University of Sanbra]], had branches located on the vessel.<ref name="JATM" />

[[File:ChuunthorCloseup-FF95.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The exterior of the ''Chu'unthor''.]]

To alleviate the sense of confinement and banality that can be experienced while traveling for long durations in a spaceship, the ''Chu'unthor'' had wide corridors with high ceilings, while many rooms had [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] domes or expansive viewports. The effect created was an environment very much like the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Great Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], rather than that of a simple starship. Classes were often held within these rooms, open to the stars, reminding the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] that a Jedi was obligated to serve [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large, rather than to use his or her gifts towards the pursuit of selfish ends.

[[File:Chuunthorskematic.jpg|200px|thumb|Schematics for the ''Chu'unthor''.]]

In the year [[230 BBY/Legends|230 BBY]], the service of the ''Chu'unthor'' was cut tragically short when it crashed into the exile world of [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]], home to Force "witches" descended from the fallen Jedi, [[Allya/Legends|Allya]]. The damaged ''Chu'unthor'' was surrounded by the witches on their [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] mounts. A group of Jedi (including [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], [[Gra'aton]], and [[Vulatan]]) were sent to attempt a rescue of the vessel's crew and passengers, but were stopped by the Witches of Dathomir. After Yoda saved the life of [[Rell (Dathomir)|Rell]], a young witch, the two were able to negotiate a settlement. The ''Chu'unthor'' and its library would be abandoned on Dathomir, but the Jedi were free to leave. Yoda asked Rell to hide the ship's reader disks until a Jedi Knight came, who would free her world from the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]], the witches who had enveloped themselves in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a second ship by the name [[Chu'unthor (Clone Wars)|''Chu'unthor'']] served as a mobile academy headed by Master [[Djinn Altis/Legends|Djinn Altis]].

Several centuries after the first ''Chu'unthor'' crashed on Dathomir, in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] came to the planet, fulfilling Yoda's prophecy. He had discovered records mentioning the rescue mission undertaken centuries earlier while visiting the planet [[Toola/Legends|Toola]], and arrived in the company of [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]] of the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], who was searching for [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and the kidnapped Princess [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. After locating Leia through the Force, Luke and Isolder discovered the ''Chu'unthor'', and began to understand the purpose of the rescue mission and found the wreckage to be “neat”. After defeating the Nightsisters and Warlord [[Zsinj]], Luke was given records<ref name="TCoPL">''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''</ref> from the ''Chu'unthor'', which he added to his collection for the [[Jedi Praxeum]] he later established on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="Dar Apprentice">[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']]</ref>

[[12 ABY/Legends|Several years later]], the ''Chu'unthor'' was ransacked by Imperial forces under the command of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vit]], as part of [[Project Nightsaber]].<ref name="Nightsaber">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031120nightsaber|text=Nightsaber|int=Nightsaber}}</ref>

==Legacy==
During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a second ship by the name [[Chu'unthor (Clone Wars)|''Chu'unthor'']] served as a mobile academy headed by Master [[Djinn Altis/Legends|Djinn Altis]].

The record on data tapes found in the wreck of the ship contained information about the training of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Later the data tapes were taken to the new Jedi library on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. In 40 ABY, nearly all of the Jedi [[Data-tape/Legends|datatapes]] in the possession of the [[New Jedi Order]] came from the Chu'unthor.<ref name="TEGTTF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>
